title: Keynote
key_points: Herndon frames AI work through two myths: the centaur (human head/inhuman body) = humans orchestrating systems with human interest at the core, vs. the minotaur (human body/beast head) = humans trapped inside systems built to model and puppeteer them.

She describes her practice as 'protocol art', the creative intervention is upstream orchestration that produces emergent media, performances, and objects downstream; the protocol and its incentives are themselves the artwork.

For 'The Call' (Serpentine Gallery, London), she wrote a songbook covering all phonemes of English, sent it to 15 UK choirs, drove around recording them with her one-year-old and mom, and trained a collective choral model so people who never met could make music together.

Holly+ (2021, a COVID project) released a model of her own singing voice for anyone to sing through, with a royalty-split incentive model, a deliberately permissive, 'positive-sum' approach to IP (e.g., Sam Rolfes singing Dolly Parton's 'Jolene' through her voice/avatar).

Installations like Star Mirror (KW Berlin, 3 months) and the Attention Guild (Palazzo Diego, Venice, May) created read/write rooms that retain memory of what happened in them, letting humans and agents sing, debate, or even write software collectively.

quotes.text: A centaur builds protocols and models, and a minotaur is trapped inside protocols built to model them.
Angels appear as what you fear, and the devil appears as what you desire.
